articles.title: U.S. Military Redirects 100 Ships In Six-Week Naval Blockade Choking Iranian Ports
articles.expand: U.S. forces have redirected 100 commercial vessels as part of its six-week military blockade of Iranian ports, Central Command said. More than 200 U.S. aircraft and warships have prevented ships from entering and exiting Iranian ports, Central Command said on Saturday. In addition to redirecting the vessels, more than 15,000 U.S. military personnel...

articles.title: A new Fed chair, an old instinct: Why markets may be misreading Warsh
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the 17th head of the Fed on Friday, the first chair to take the oath at the White House since Alan Greenspan in 1987, a venue choice that says plenty about how close this central bank now sits to the executive branch. The optics got stranger from there.

articles.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average shrugs off a hawkish new Fed chair to set a record
articles.expand: There is confident, and then there is Friday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) ground out a fresh all-time high, up around a quarter of a percent, on a day that gave it nothing to celebrate.

articles.title: Gold holds within weekly range as US-Iran talks remain in focus
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) consolidates on Friday, remaining confined within this week’s trading range as investors cautiously monitor ongoing diplomatic efforts between the United States and Iran to reach a deal to end the war in the Middle East.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar weakens as US-Iran uncertainty and hawkish Fed bets support Greenback
articles.expand: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) remains on the defensive against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday as traders look past stronger Canadian Retail Sales figures and keep their attention on developments surrounding US-Iran negotiations.

articles.title: Canada: Growth returns with domestic support – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) economists Nathan Janzen and Claire Fan expect Canada’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to rebound in Q1 2026, projecting a 1.7% annualized expansion after a 0.6% contraction in Q4.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Tokyo CPI and intervention data watched – Deutsche Bank
articles.expand: Deutsche Bank analysts expect Japan’s Tokyo Consumer Price Index (CPI) to show a slight acceleration, with core CPI ex fresh food seen at 1.6% YoY and core-core at 2.0%. Industrial production is forecast to rise 0.2% MoM.
